{{Youmay|Official Star Wars Fan Club Newsletter|[[Homing Beacon (newsletter)|Homing Beacon]] (the StarWars.com newsletter), or the fictional [[Bantha Tracks|Batuu business]]}}

"'''Bantha Tracks'''" originally known as the '''Official Star Wars Fan Club Newsletter''', was a newsletter published from [[1978]] to [[1987]]. It is currently a department of ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine that focuses on ''[[Star Wars]]'' fans. [[Pete Vilmur]] became the department's editor beginning with [[Star Wars Insider 157|issue 157]], succeeding [[Mary Franklin]], the department's original editor dating back to [[2002]].

==Release and reception==
Its original incarnation, the Official Star Wars Fan Club Newsletter, was distributed to members every two months; ''Bantha Tracks'' was distributed quarterly. The first two years of ''Bantha Tracks'' were edited and mainly written by [[Craig Miller (Lucasfilm)|Craig Miller]], who was Director of Fan Relations for Lucasfilm from [[1977]] to [[1980]].

The name ''Bantha Tracks'' was decided in a contest from [[Official Star Wars Fan Club 2|the Official Star Wars Fan Club Newsletter #2]] which allowed fan club members to submit ideas for a less literal name. The winner was Preston Postle of [[Wikipedia:Avon, Ohio|Avon, Ohio]], who suggested the name ''Bantha Tracks''. Runner-up names included ''The Comlink'' and ''Inside Star Wars''.

The original ''Bantha Tracks'' ran for 35 issues until its discontinuation in 1987.

In 2003, [[StarWars.com]] released all 35 issues online for [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] members from June to December as '''Classic Bantha Tracks'''. And in [[2004]], they also introduced a new, biweekly, online publication known as '''Bantha Tracks Online''', which remained in publication until at least August [[2005]].

Today, "Bantha Tracks" is a section in ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' that displays fans' art and picture submissions.
